summary: Small tooling PR that added the `--passthru-magic-macros` option to the `pcpp` argument list used by `tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py`. The PR was closed unmerged shortly after creation, with the author noting it was probably not the best solution and pointing to #920.

key_files.reason: Only changed file; added one `pcpp` command-line argument to the m2ctx preprocessing configuration.

what_changed: Added `--passthru-magic-macros` to the list of arguments passed to `pcpp` in `tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py`.
The existing argument list already included macro-related options such as `-D MUST_MATCH`, `-U FIGHTERVARS_SIZE`; this PR extended that list by preserving magic macros through preprocessing.
The PR was closed without merge.
smart_moves: Targeted the m2ctx preprocessing layer directly instead of changing generated context output or downstream source files.
Kept the patch minimal: one added command-line argument, making the behavioral change easy to inspect and revert.
Identified a specific `pcpp` option relevant to preserving special predefined/magic macro behavior during context generation.
decomp_lessons: For decomp tooling fixes, prefer adjusting context-generation/preprocessor flags when the issue is caused by preprocessing behavior rather than modifying source headers to compensate.
Very small tool patches can still materially affect matching workflow if they alter what macros survive into generated context.
Closed or superseded tooling PRs are useful evidence of attempted approaches, but should not be treated as endorsed project practice without checking the follow-up PR.
naming_conventions: `m2ctx.py` is the tool script name used for m2c context generation.
`MUST_MATCH` appears as a macro passed through the m2ctx/pcpp preprocessing setup.
`FIGHTERVARS_SIZE` appears as a macro explicitly undefined with `-U` in the existing preprocessing argument list.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use preprocessor configuration to preserve macro information needed by downstream decompilation/context tooling.

When generated m2c context is affected by predefined or magic macros, consider `pcpp` flags such as `--passthru-magic-macros` as a possible tactic, while validating against project-approved follow-up fixes.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.text: Probably not the best solution, see #920.
review_feedback.interpretation: The author explicitly marked this approach as likely inferior or superseded by PR #920; future work should inspect #920 before reusing this exact fix.

follow_up_queries: Inspect PR #920 to understand the preferred replacement for passing `--passthru-magic-macros` to `pcpp`.
Search current `tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py` for `--passthru-magic-macros` to see whether the option was later adopted, removed, or replaced.
Check m2ctx context-generation failures involving magic macros or predefined macros before reusing this closed PR's approach.
